name: Pererenan (峇里島 - 佩雷雷南 / Pantai Pererenan)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: 24 km (approx. 75-95 min by car depending on traffic)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 8-12 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 80-105 min by scooter
wave_type: Mixed beach and reef break - A-frame peak with both left and right; left is longer with two or three turns then closes on inside reef shelf, right is shorter, steeper and more intense; some Rivermouth-style section just north
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Pererenan main peak); high-level intermediates comfortable with shallow bottoms
wave_height_range: 2-8 ft (best head-high to slightly overhead; closes out over 1.5x overhead)
bottom_type: Mixed - sand on the outside section/shore break; volcanic lava rock and reef on the inside, especially on the left
best_season: Dry season May-October prime (consistent SW swell and offshore NE/SE winds). April and November shoulder months. Wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and murky.
best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid is sweet spot, high tide best shape over the volcanic rock shelf). Low tide exposes sharp jagged lava reef - avoid unless very confident.
best_wind: Offshore from Northeast / East / Southeast (NE in early morning, switching to SE trade winds); turns onshore by 10-11am
best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and WSW groundswells
swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 (receives the same swell as Canggu/Echo; very consistent in dry season)
wave_length_meters: 30-80 m (left often 50-80 m before closing on inside reef; right typically 20-40 m) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Medium-fast (left is more relaxed, right is intense and punchy; rewards shortboards and good rail surfing)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to Difficult - steep but readable A-frame takeoff; sand outside is forgiving, but the inside left ends over a shallow sharp rock shelf so you must kick out early
secondary_peaks: Pererenan main peak (A-frame), Pererenan Rivermouth (immediately south toward Echo) which is a longer left with more shape, plus a few inside reform peaks and visible reefs further out; sandbar peaks shift along the beach
peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - generally less crowded than Echo Left or Batu Bolong but can still see 30-40 surfers at peak dawn-patrol on good days; spreads out more along the bay
access: Drive down Jalan Pantai Pererenan / Jl. Pantai Munduk to the dedicated beach parking area right in front of the break; short walk over sand to the lineup
paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the beach through the shore break and into the channel just south of the statue / rivermouth; on bigger days the channel between Pererenan main peak and Rivermouth offers easier paddle out
entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 2,000-5,000 scooter; IDR 5,000-10,000 car at official Pererenan lots.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
best_time_of_day: Before 7-8am to avoid both onshore wind and the local + tourist crowd that builds quickly when waves are good.
nearby_warung: Warung Heboh (right in front of the break, great view, prices from IDR 10,000), Warung Alus (BBQ seafood, steaks, burgers), La Brisa nearby in Echo, Pererenan local warungs along Jl. Pantai Pererenan, coconut and snack stalls on the beach
alternative_spot_within_10min: Echo Beach Left (5 min south), Echo Right / Sandbar (5 min south), Batu Bolong (8-10 min south for smaller mellower waves), Seseh (5-10 min north for less crowded alternative), Mengening (10 min north)
hazards: Shallow sharp volcanic lava rock shelf on the inside left; rocks and rips at the rivermouth; tide changes can be violent and unpredictable; rip currents in the channel near the rivermouth; crowd can be heavy at peak times; bacterial water after rain due to nearby river-mouth
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the volcanic rock and reef inside (carry vinegar/tweezers); rare jellyfish in wet season; occasional stingrays in shallows - shuffle feet; rocks rather than coral so marine life is limited; bacterial issues from river runoff in wet season
reef_cut_severity_index: Medium to High - lava rock shelf on the inside left is sharp; reef cuts common on low-tide wipeouts; can be deep enough to require treatment but generally less severe than Padang Padang or Uluwatu
rip_current_pattern: Channel rip running just south of the main peak toward the rivermouth - useful for paddling out but pulls strongly on outgoing tide; side-sweeping current along the inside reef toward the rivermouth on bigger swells
lineup_etiquette: Mellower than Echo Beach Left or Batu Bolong; priority on the peak still expected; wait your turn; don't snake or back-paddle; greet and nod to locals; respect Indonesian regulars
localism_intensity: Low to Medium - Pererenan is amidst a traditional Balinese community with strong spiritual ties; vibe is generally mellow but locals do enforce priority on the peak; aggression is rare if etiquette is followed
respect_protocol: Sit wide and take your turn; don't paddle inside locals; cover up walking through the village (sarong/shirt); respect ceremonies and offerings (canang sari) on the sand; tip warungs and parking attendants modestly
resident_local_legends: Putu Swastika 'Gemuk' (founder of Surfers Surf Lesson at Pererenan), local Balinese surfers from the village shred here daily; international free-surfers often visit. [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Pererenan sits in a traditional Balinese village with multiple temples (pura) and active ceremonial life. Respect ceremonies, do not step on offerings (canang sari) on the sand, observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing or beach access; women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds.
medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu, ~10 min), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar.
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~4 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~28 km; Siloam Denpasar ~18 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at Pererenan; Balawista lifeguards occasionally cover the area; surf school instructors provide informal rescues
emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
nearest_surf_school: Surfers Surf School (Putu Swastika 'Gemuk' - private school directly at Pererenan), 360 Surf Travel (Pererenan-based surf camp), CARI Surf Camp (in-house photographer), Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, Mondo Surf Village; many Canggu-based schools also bring students here
board_rental_availability: On-beach and on-road rental available at multiple shops along Jl. Pantai Pererenan; soft-tops, longboards, performance shortboards all available; daily rates similar to Canggu
surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot the Pererenan peak daily during dry season; many surf schools have in-house photographers (CARI, Surfers Surf School)
photographer_recommendation: Surfers Surf School in-house photo/video package; freelance photographers shooting Pererenan via Instagram/QR code; typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Pererenan has a wide range from cheap guesthouses to luxury villas, surf camps (360 Surf Travel, Mondo Surf Village, Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu, CARI), boutique villas, and traditional village homestays; calmer and cheaper alternative to central Canggu
webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Pererenan/Echo area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth / Pererenan-adjacent cam) [uncertain]
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography (DGCA registration required for >250g). Avoid flying over the village temples, low over crowded lineup, or near ceremonies. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
coworking_nearby: Yes - Outpost Canggu (15 min), Dojo Bali (10-15 min), B Work Bali (Nelayan, 10-12 min), Tropical Nomad; growing number of villa coworking setups in Pererenan itself.
avg_lesson_price: IDR 400,000-700,000 (~USD 26-45 / TWD 800-1,400) per session at local Pererenan surf schools
board_rental_daily: Soft-top / longboard IDR 75,000-100,000/day; performance shortboard IDR 100,000-200,000/day from on-beach and Jl. Pantai Pererenan shops
water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to rivermouth and village drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
annual_competition_window: No major WSL event; occasional local Indonesian Surfing Championship Tour (ISC) regional events and free-surf sessions; informal contests run by local schools [uncertain]
community_events: Beach clean-ups by Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean, ceremonial Hindu processions to the sea (Melasti before Nyepi), full moon ceremonies, surf-camp-organized BBQs and yoga sessions
